title: When is Amazon Prime Day 2026? The official dates, plus more about the 4-day sale
summary: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening from June 23 to 26, and only Prime members get access. Here’s what you need to know and what’s new about the sale this year.
story: It’s official: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening from June 23 to 26. The Prime member-exclusive sale typically offers some of the lowest prices of the year on thousands of products across categories like tech, clothing, beauty, kitchen and more, and you can save on essentials like groceries and household staples too.

To help you plan for the sale, I answered some important questions below — this is my seventh year covering Prime Day, so I know all the ins and outs, and I’ve been talking to experts for weeks about their expectations. Read on for everything you need to know and bookmark this article: I’ll share frequent updates as I learn more about Prime Day 2026.
Want more from NBC Select? Sign up for our newsletter, The Selection, and shop smarter.
When is Prime Day 2026?
Amazon Prime Day 2026 is from June 23 to 26. Amazon hosting the sale in June is earlier than usual, although not unheard of — the event is typically in July, but Amazon hosted it in June in 2021.
How long is Prime Day 2026?
Amazon Prime Day 2026 is four days long. The sale typically took place across two days, but in 2025, Amazon extended it to four days, which may be the norm going forward.
Do you need to be a Prime member to shop on Prime Day?

Yes, only Prime members get access to Prime Day deals. You can sign up for a free 30-day trial of the Prime membership (learn about the three different types here) on Amazon’s website or app. Amazon automatically charges you for your Prime membership once the free trial period ends.
Can you shop early Prime Day deals?

Yes, Prime members can shop early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals as the event approaches — some are even live right now. The best way to stay updated about early Prime Day deals is to download the Amazon app, which can notify you about sales in real-time.
What are the best deals to shop on Prime Day?

Products across all shopping categories are on sale during Prime Day, but you won’t know exactly what’s available until the event starts. In my experience covering Amazon Prime Day, the best deals usually center around Amazon-owned technology like Echo, Fire, Blink and Ring devices, small home and kitchen appliances, beauty products, apparel and back-to-school supplies. You’ll also be able to shop Amazon’s Lightning Deals during the event: They’re flash sales that give shoppers a short window to purchase limited quantities of specific products.

I recommend browsing through Amazon’s early Prime Day deals to get a sense of what’s going to be discounted, and looking at Prime Day bestsellers from previous years. In 2025, for example. NBC Select readers’ favorite deals included the Shark Navigator Life-Away Vacuum, Crest 3D Professional Effects Whitestrips, Apple AirPods 4, Cascade Platinum Dishwasher Pods and Bissell Little Green Multi-Purpose Portable Carpet and Upholstery Cleaner.
What’s new about Amazon Prime Day 2026?
In addition to pushing the sale up to June, Amazon is running new programs and sweepstakes during Prime Day 2026 that you should have on your radar.

Each day of the sale, Amazon is running its Today’s Big Deals program, during which it’s dropping new discounts (many at 50 percent off or more) at midnight, 8 a.m. and 1 p.m. PT.Amazon is adding new deals to its site as often as every five minutes during certain periods throughout the event.Prime members get access to exclusive travel deals, like up to 30 percent off Budget and Avis rentals (plus 10 percent back in an Amazon gift card), and 10 percent off Chicago hotels.At Whole Foods Market, Prime members can get an extra 10 percent off sale items online and in-store, and exclusive in-store deals every Tuesday and Friday. Prime members who spend $15 or more on a qualifying online grocery  order have a chance to win free groceries for a year. Amazon is giving away $1 million in total prizes across 100 winners, who will be announced weekly leading up to Prime Day.Prime members who set up a deal alert with Alexa have a chance to win a $1,000 Amazon gift card. The retailer will select 100 winners for its Alexa for Shopping Picks Up the Tab sweepstakes by Prime Day.From Jun. 15 to 26, Prime members can get a large pepperoni or cheese pizza from Little Caesars for $5. This offer is redeemable up to five times on different days for savings of up to $30, depending on location.From Jun. 11 to Jul. 9, Prime members can get a $200 Amazon gift card upon approval for a Prime Visa, or a $120 Amazon gift card upon approval for a Prime Store Card. Prime cardholders earn extra cashback (7 percent) on eligible purchases during Prime Day when they choose no-rush delivery.  From Jun. 2 to 26, Prime members who purchase a new car through Amazon Autos get a $1,500 Amazon gift card.Since Prime Day is happening before July Fourth, the World Cup and Father’s Day, expect to see themed deals around these big events.
Each day of the sale, Amazon is running its Today’s Big Deals program, during which it’s dropping new discounts (many at 50 percent off or more) at midnight, 8 a.m. and 1 p.m. PT.
Amazon is adding new deals to its site as often as every five minutes during certain periods throughout the event.
Prime members get access to exclusive travel deals, like up to 30 percent off Budget and Avis rentals (plus 10 percent back in an Amazon gift card), and 10 percent off Chicago hotels.
At Whole Foods Market, Prime members can get an extra 10 percent off sale items online and in-store, and exclusive in-store deals every Tuesday and Friday.

Prime members who spend $15 or more on a qualifying online grocery  order have a chance to win free groceries for a year. Amazon is giving away $1 million in total prizes across 100 winners, who will be announced weekly leading up to Prime Day.

Prime members who set up a deal alert with Alexa have a chance to win a $1,000 Amazon gift card. The retailer will select 100 winners for its Alexa for Shopping Picks Up the Tab sweepstakes by Prime Day.

From Jun. 15 to 26, Prime members can get a large pepperoni or cheese pizza from Little Caesars for $5. This offer is redeemable up to five times on different days for savings of up to $30, depending on location.

From Jun. 11 to Jul. 9, Prime members can get a $200 Amazon gift card upon approval for a Prime Visa, or a $120 Amazon gift card upon approval for a Prime Store Card. Prime cardholders earn extra cashback (7 percent) on eligible purchases during Prime Day when they choose no-rush delivery.
From Jun. 2 to 26, Prime members who purchase a new car through Amazon Autos get a $1,500 Amazon gift card.
Since Prime Day is happening before July Fourth, the World Cup and Father’s Day, expect to see themed deals around these big events.
What is Amazon Prime?

Amazon Prime is the retailer’s loyalty program. You can try it out with a 30-day free trial, after that, a standard membership costs $139 a year or $14.99 a month. Amazon also offers two types of discounted accounts: Prime for Young Adults, which is $7.49 a month for those 18 to 24 years old, and Prime Access, which is $3.50 for the first three months, and then $6.99 a month for those who qualify for certain government assistance programs.

Benefits of a Prime membership include access to Prime Day deals and free  two-day, one-day and same-day delivery. Prime members also get a handful of streaming and entertainment perks, plus they can order groceries online through Whole Foods or Amazon Fresh and sign up for a free Grubhub+ membership.
Is Prime Day twice a year?

Amazon has hosted two Prime Day-level events every year since 2022: Amazon Prime Day during the summer (typically July, but it’s in June this year) and Prime Big Deal Days during the fall (typically in October, often referred to by shoppers as October Prime Day). So far, the retailer has only confirmed this summer’s Prime Day. We won’t find out if it’s hosting Prime Big Deal Days until later this year.
What countries are participating in Prime Day?

There are 26 countries participating in Prime Day 2026. Members in the following countries are eligible to shop in June: Austria, Belgium, Canada, Colombia, Egypt, France, Germany, Italy, Ireland, Luxembourg, Mexico, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Saudi Arabia, Singapore, Spain, Sweden, Türkiye, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom and the United States. Later in the summer, Amazon is hosting Prime Day in Australia, Brazil, India and Japan.
Why trust NBC Select?

I’m a reporter at NBC Select who has covered Amazon since 2020. In addition to numerous sales stories, I wrote NBC Select’s history of Amazon Prime Day and Prime membership guide, both of which I frequently update. I also regularly appear in related NBC News NOW and TODAY segments. For this article, I researched Prime Day 2026, reviewed information from Amazon press releases and spoke to Amazon executives on background.
Catch up on NBC Select’s in-depth coverage of tech and tools, wellness and more, and follow us on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and TikTok to stay up to date.

title: Brendan Banfield receives life sentence in elaborate au pair double murder plot
summary: The former IRS agent convicted in a deadly catfishing scheme targeting his wife and a stranger was sentenced Friday to a mandatory life term in prison
story: The former IRS agent convicted in a deadly catfishing scheme targeting his wife and a stranger was sentenced Friday to a mandatory life term in prison.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Brendan Banfield, 40, was found guilty in February on two counts of aggravated murder, one count of using a firearm in committing a felony, and child endangerment in the 2023 killings of Christine Banfield and Joseph Ryan at the Banfields’ home in Herndon, Virginia.

The couple’s au pair involved in the plot, Juliana Peres Magalhaes, pleaded guilty to manslaughter and was sentenced to 10 years in prison. Peres Magalhaes initially faced charges of second-degree murder but was given the lesser punishment after she cooperated with authorities and testified for the prosecution at Banfield’s trial in Northern Virginia.
On Friday, Judge Penney Azcarate sentenced Banfield to life in the penitentiary without the possibility of parole.

“The level of cruelty, calculation, and inhumanity in this case reflects something far deeper than anger or impulse; it reflects evil, which is why I carry no burden and find no hesitation in sentencing you to life,” the judge said.
Banfield has the right to file an appeal within 30 days.

Christine Banfield’s sister, Danielle, spoke directly to convicted murderer Brendan Banfield while offering her impact statement, telling Banfield that his daughter, whom he and Christine shared, will come to terms with the fact that he put her in harm’s way.

“He could have divorced and moved on, but divorce would have required relinquishing control, and control was always the priority. His actions were not driven by love, but a desire for power, deception, and a complete disregard for the lies he destroyed.”
She stood crying at the podium in a trembling voice, saying that her bond with her sister would never go away and remembering her as a caring person.
Joseph Ryan’s mother, Deirdre Fisher, appeared in the court virtually due to medical reasons.
“Brendan will remain known as an abusive father, the brutal murderer of his dedicated and compassionate, beautiful wife, and a narcissistic killer of an innocent man,” she said.
Brendan Banfield spoke in the court before his sentencing was announced, stating that he was “greatly disappointed in the legal system”.
He continued, saying that the prosecution and their witnesses’ statements do not match the evidence and that the Fairfax County Police Department  “never truly looked into Joe Ryan.”

“I loved her very much, despite what you may think of my affairs. Our marriage worked for us.” Banfield added, “She truly was a caring, a caring mother, caring wife, loving nurse. But I am not responsible. I am not responsible for her death.”
During the trial, Banfield admitted on the stand that he cheated on his wife with Magalhaes, 25, and with several other women.
Prosecutors said Banfield was motivated by an affair and used the fetish website to lure Ryan, 39, to what he believed was a violent sexual fantasy at the Banfields’ home Feb. 24, 2023.
The events of that morning ended with Banfield fatally stabbing his wife, 37, and shooting Ryan, prosecutors said.

In court, Peres Magalhaes said she also shot Ryan. She said Banfield had been trying to avoid a divorce when they embarked on the catfishing plot and used Christine Banfield’s laptop to create a fake user profile on the fetish site that purported to be her.
The profile, “Annastasia9,” described wanting to find a stranger with whom she could act out the sexual fantasy in her home. Ryan was that stranger, Peres Magalhaes said.
During his trial, Banfield rejected those allegations and called them “absolutely crazy.” He testified that he shot Ryan after he saw him stab his wife.
“I did not want to shoot him,” said Banfield, who testified that he was armed with his service weapon. “Um, I wanted him to let her go.”

title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing gold bars ordered to remain in jail pending trial
summary: ALEXANDRIA, Va. — The former CIA officer found with $40 million in gold bars stashed at his Virginia home was ordered held behind bars Friday until he goes on trial for criminal theft of public money after a prosecutor branded him a “master manipulator.”David Rush, a senior CIA officer who worked on one of the most highly sensitive programs in the U.S. government, watched as prosecutors and his defense attorney sparred in a Virginia courtroom over whether he should remain in jail
story: ALEXANDRIA, Va. — The former CIA officer found with $40 million in gold bars stashed at his Virginia home was ordered held behind bars Friday until he goes on trial for criminal theft of public money after a prosecutor branded him a “master manipulator.”
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

David Rush, a senior CIA officer who worked on one of the most highly sensitive programs in the U.S. government, watched as prosecutors and his defense attorney sparred in a Virginia courtroom over whether he should remain in jail.
Magistrate Judge William Fitzpatrick sided with the government and ordered that Rush remain detained in the Alexandria Detention Center.
“He is in a different position than most people,” Fitzpatrick said, noting his risk of flight and the high level of danger he could pose to the community.
Looking unshaven and wearing a dark green jumpsuit with “Alexandria inmate” emblazoned on the back, Rush did not show any reaction.
Rush, 49, has also been accused by the government of lying about his work experience and education for nearly two decades, according to court records on file in the Eastern District of Virginia.
Assistant U.S. Attorney Gavin Tisdale called Rush a “master manipulator” and a “tremendous fraud” who lied about nearly every aspect of his professional career.

Rush’s attorney Jessica Carmichael, arguing that her client should be released pending trial, focused on his only being charged with one count of theft of public funds and his cooperation with the FBI when they searched his home. Every gold bar Rush allegedly took from the CIA has been accounted for, prosecutors said.
“This is about $65,000 worth of time card fraud,” Carmichael said.
She noted that her client was being held in solitary confinement due to media attention surrounding his case and said he is only allowed out for two hours a day.
Rush was arrested on May 19 after FBI agents raided his Virginia home and found about 303 gold bars, $2 million in cash and more than 30 luxury watches, according to the court affidavit.

It wasn’t immediately clear when Rush left the agency, but in the wake of his arrest there has been a fallout at the agency. The CIA put several senior officials who handled the high-ranking officer on administrative leave.

Meanwhile, the arrest has raised questions about how effective the government has been at vetting applicants for jobs like the management position that Rush held, which comes with top-secret-level clearance.
Rush is accused of filing fraudulent time sheets by falsely claiming he was a member of the Navy Reserve.
Between November and March of this year, Rush had requested and received from the CIA a large amount of foreign currency and gold bars for “work-related expenses,” the court papers say.
But, according to the papers, when government officials checked Rush’s office they found that “only a portion of the currency he received remained in the storage space.”
A CIA employee for about 17 years, Rush was most recently a liaison to the Defense Department for a highly sensitive nuclear submarine program, NBC News has reported.

Rush was given that assignment at the request of Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg, with whom Rush had a close professional relationship over the years, according to four people familiar with the two men.
Feinberg, who has not been accused of wrongdoing, was a major donor to President Donald Trump’s campaigns and founded the private equity firm Cerberus Capital Management.
Chief Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell has denied that Rush and Feinberg had “a close relationship of any kind.”
In a previous statement to NBC News, Parnell said that Feinberg “never supported Mr. Rush’s career at any point in his life, nor did he endorse Mr. Rush for any career position.”
Rush applied to work with the government three times.
In his first application, Rush falsely claimed he graduated from Clemson University in 2000. In his second application, he added that he had a graduate degree from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.

On his third try, in 2009, he succeeded, and he included those degrees and an aircraft test from the U.S. Naval Test Pilot School, according to court documents. In applications for promotions, he said he’d been a thesis adviser at the Air Force Institute of Technology. Rush also told employers he was a pilot for the Navy.

None of it was true, according to the charges; he didn’t graduate from the schools, and investigators say the Federal Aviation Administration doesn’t have a certificate or a pilot’s license registered to Rush.

title: Karmelo Anthony jury views video showing chaotic aftermath of fatal stabbing
summary: Tensions were high as the second day of testimony in the murder trial of a Texas teenager accused of stabbing to death a high schooler at a track meet got underway Friday.
story: McKINNEY, Texas — Tensions ran high as the second day of testimony got underway Friday in the murder trial of a Karmelo Anthony, the Texas teenager accused of fatally stabbing a high schooler at a track meet.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Three people were removed from the Collin County courthouse before morning proceedings began. A woman directed harsh words at a sheriff’s deputy, a blogger from North Carolina was removed for allegedly calling deputy a racial slur, and a third person may have been violating courthouse protocols prohibiting cell phone use and recording in the building.
Anthony, 19, who is Black, is charged with fatally stabbing Austin Metcalf, 17, who is white, in the chest in a dispute over a seat at a track meet in Frisco, Texas, April 2, 2025.

Anthony has pleaded not guilty. Anthony’s attorney Mike Howard said Thursday in opening arguments that the stabbing was in self defense, but Texas prosecutor Bill Wirskye said the case was “unjustified provoked murder.”
Anthony was 17 at the time of the stabbing but in Texas, 17-year-olds are considered adults. If Anthony is found guilty, he could face five years to life in state prison.
The case has drawn national attention, in part because of the racial dynamics. The makeup of the jury, with no Black members, has drawn criticism from some group and trial watchers.

Texas state district judge John Roach Jr. issued a stern warning to the public attending the trial at the start of Friday’s proceedings. He warned them they are not allowed to talk and “if myself sees you do that they are authorized to escort you out and you’re not allowed to come back.”

Early in Friday’s proceedings, state prosecutor Wirskey showed a four-minute video of Anthony’s arrest, part of which was captured by the body camera worn by Officer Eduardo Cortez, the school resource officer for Frisco police department.
Cortez testified that he was at a nearby middle school when the call went out over the radio of a stabbing, with a description of the suspect.
In the video, Anthony is seen holding his hands over his head while the officer tells others over his radio  that he has located the alleged suspect.
Anthony, who is barely audible in the video clip, makes a pleading comment. Cortez had testified minutes before that Anthony had said, “I’m not alleged. I did it.”
“He put his hands on me. I told him not to. He put his hands on me,” Anthony said.

Anthony’s emotion became a point of argument in the trial over whether Anthony was crying or was calm and cool. Cortez testified that he had described Anthony as emotional in his report, but that description only applied to when Anthony said he warned Metcalf not to put his hands on him.
Anthony spelled his name for officers in the video and they noted a small bit of blood on the finger of one of his cuffed hands.
“I go to Centennial (High School). I’m in 12th grade,” he said in the video.
A second officer approached them and they escorted Anthony to the police cruiser. Anthony continued talking.
“When we placed him in the squad car, he asked if he was going to be ok. I told him I don’t know,” Cortez said.
Cortez said Anthony was cooperative.
Maria Guerrero reported from McKinney, Texas and Suzanne Gamboa from Austin, Texas.

title: ‘Mexico 1986’ Star Diego Luna Breaks Down the True Story That Inspired the Film
summary: Diego Luna’s new Netflix movie, “Mexico 1986,” is loosely based on how Mexico obtained the World Cup 40 years ago.  Here is the true story behind the movie and if Martin de la Torre is a real person.
story: This June, the 2026 FIFA World Cup will take place in three countries, the United States, Mexico and Canada.

Forty-eight teams will give it their all for a chance to represent their countries and secure the top soccer trophy. This year marks the first year the U.S. is hosting the World Cup since 1994, but 40 years ago, Mexico fought hard to be the sole location of the tournament.

The story is seemingly depicted in “Mexico 86,” a new Spanish-language Netflix movie starring Diego Luna, which tells the dramatized story of how against all odds the country came out on top to obtain the event.

“I got involved with this film before the script was even finished,” Luna, who also serves as an executive producer, tells TODAY.com in Spanish. “I got involved because of the promise of what the project represented.”
A true underdog story and one that sees Mexico triumph, “Mexico 86” is directed by Gabriel Ripstein, with Karla Souza, Daniel Giménez Cacho, Davor Tomic and Álvaro Guerrero co-starring.
With another World Cup on its hand, Mexico is the only country to host the tournament three times. Read on to learn what went down in 1986 and what is fact and fiction in “Mexico 86.”
Is ‘Mexico 86’ Based on a True Story?
Yes, “Mexico 86” is loosely based on the events of how Mexico won the bid to host the 1986 World Cup.

The North American country first hosted in 1970, and was not set to host the tournament in 1986. It was originally set to take place in Colombia, but due to economic factors the nation had to withdraw.
FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association) had to scramble to find a new host with many nations, including the U.S., Canada and Mexico, hoping to secure the event.

A 21-member executive committee made the “unanimous decision” for Mexico to host after Canada reportedly “offered too few stadiums for the tournament and that the United States Government had not provided sufficient logistical guarantees,” The New York Times reported in 1983.

U.S. Secretary of State Henry A. Kissinger had been working to secure the nation as host, but ultimately the FIFA president at the time, João Havelange, selected Mexico. According to the Times, FIFA “refused to send inspecting delegations” to the U.S. and Canada, after inspecting facilities in Mexico.
“Mexico 86” shows at one point, how vice president of FIFA Hermann Neuberger visited Mexico and the country’s bid became official.
However, it wasn’t an easy task to pull off. In 1985, Mexico City suffered a devastating earthquake that killed more than 12,000 people and had to rebuild the city, the Associated Press reported. The

As the film notes, in 1986, Mexico was in an economic crisis of its own with unemployment and the public disillusioned with politicians. But after securing the World Cup, for a month that year, Mexico “felt like the center of the universe.”
“Mexico 86” specifically shows how one determined man, Martín de la Torre, did everything in his power to get Mexico the World Cup.
Is Martín de la Torre a Real Person?
Described as a “cunning Mexican bureaucrat” in the film’s synopsis, Martín de la Torre is not a real person.
Luna shares that de la Torre is a culmination of “many executives from that time.”

“We didn’t want to commit to a specific name so we could include all the anecdotes we’d heard, the ones we know happened, fill in the blanks,” the actor says, adding they also wanted to touch on  “legendary stories, the ones that who knows if they actually happened but are still told.”
Luna says that de la Torre represents the Mexican Football Federation.

“Many things we know happened with the Federation, in this case, we attributed to the character,” he explains. “And we wanted the freedom to also tell a fictional story, because it’s not a documentary or a journalistic report, it’s not a journalistic investigation.”
He says it’s a fun “film based on real events, on things we know happened and others that we invented.”
His approach when creating de la Torre, he says, was to portray a “man full of contradictions, vices, whose actions are questionable.”
“He’s neither good nor bad, he lives in between and is trying to be the best version of himself,” Luna says. “But, his vices end up betraying him.”
The result is an enjoyable and comical tale of underdogs doing what they have to to get the job done.
What Happened at the 1986 World Cup?
In short, Mexico hosted 24 teams for the 1986 World Cup. In the final, held at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City on June 29, were Argentina and West Germany.
Argentina took home their second World Cup title, winning 3-2. The man leading Argentina was Diego Maradona, considered one of the greatest soccer players of all time.
The 2026 World Cup kicks off in Mexico on June 11 with the star0-studded opening ceremony and match between Mexico and South Africa.

title: Anthony Head, Who Played Giles in ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer,’ Dies at 72
summary: Anthony Head, the actor best known for his work on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso,” has died.
story: Anthony Head, the actor best known for his work on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso,” has died. He was 72.
“He passed away peacefully of complications due to pneumonia, surrounded by his family,” his daughters Emily and Daisy said in a statement to the Press Association news agency.
“It has been, and forever will be, an honour and a privilege to be his daughters, and to have witnessed firsthand the impact both he and his work have had on so many,” they added.
The British actor was most recently known for his work on “Ted Lasso,” playing Rupert Mannion, the nasty ex-husband of AFC Richmond owner Rebecca Welton, portrayed by Hannah Waddingham.

Head appeared in several British shows before breaking through on American television in the 1990s playing Rupert Giles, Buffy’s watcher in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” which aired from 1997 until 2003.

Head appeared in 121 of the series’ 144 episodes. One of his signature moments came in a 1997 Season 2 episode entitled “Lie to Me,” in which Buffy (Sarah Michelle Gellar) is feeling beaten down and glum about life.
“Does it ever get easy?” Buffy asks.
“You mean life?” Giles replies.
“Yeah. Does it get easy?” she says.
“What do you want me to say?” he replies.
“Lie to me,” she says.

“Yes, it’s terribly simple,” he says. “The good guys are always stalwart and true. The bad guys are easily distinguished by their pointy horns or black hats. And we always defeat them and save the day. No one ever dies and everybody lives happily ever after.”
“Liar,” she says when he finishes.
Head is the second notable “Buffy” star to die in under a year and a half. In February 2025, Michelle Trachtenberg died at the age of 39 of complications from diabetes.

Head also appeared in such shows as “Doctor Who,” “Dominion,” “Robot Chicken,” “Jack Ryan,” “Highlander,” “NYPD Blue” and “Bridgerton,” as well as the movies “The Iron Lady” and “Ghost Rider: The Spirit of Vengeance.”
His last credit was 2024’s “Upgraded,” according to IMDB.

title: Taylor Swift Paints Ultimate Nostalgia With New Country-Pop Song ‘I Knew It, I Knew You.’ Read the Lyrics
summary: Taylor Swift’s new original single, “I Knew It, I Knew You,” released on Friday, June 5, is a full-circle moment for fans who have grown up with the “Toy Story” franchise and Swift’s stardom
story: Taylor Swift’s new original single, “I Knew It, I Knew You,” released on Friday, June 5, is a full-circle moment for fans who have grown up with the “Toy Story” franchise and Swift’s stardom.

After Easter eggs sparked anticipation across social media, Swift confirmed her involvement in the soundtrack on June 1 and later shared on Instagram her own connection to the movie franchise: A love for the character Jessie.

“Writing this song felt like a musical departure and coming home at the same time,” she said on Instagram to celebrate the song’s release. “Creating something for Jessie was a new challenge and also felt like second nature all at once. And being a @toystory kid from the age of 5 til now… is an adventure I plan to be on, to infinity and beyond.”
Through a series of imagery-filled reflections, “I Knew It, I Knew You” depicts the irreplaceable joy of reconnecting with an estranged friend, romantic partner or something from childhood.
The song starts with not a lavender haze, but a “daze of the blades of the grass in the summer,” in typical wordsmith Swift fashion.
Most of the “Toy Story” movies feature the summer season, from the first movie in 1995 to “Toy Story 4,” where the toys go on a summer road trip.
With a cheery country-meets-electropop sound (the touch of composer Jack Antonoff), plus some emotionally heavy lyrics, the track emulates complex up-and-downs in a relationship that come with time.
“What I thought would be the last time I saw/ my friend/ But love has ways of bringing thinks back to life, Swift sings on the track.

The lyrics are also full of rich imagery. The 14-time Grammy winner has a reputation for color metaphors. In this new track, she sings, “All your blues like a mood ring changing colors,” bringing to mind her iconic chorus led by, “Losing him was blue,” on the title track of 2012’s “Red” album.
For the backbeat, a harmonica leads the way in the instrumentals, leading some to celebrate, as one fan put it, the return of “Taycountry,” fitting for the character of cowgirl Jessie.

Not much is known about the plot of “Toy Story 5,” which hits theaters June 19. The logline previews the beloved cast of characters facing “what kids are obsessed with today... electronics!” However, Joan Cusack, who voices Jessie, has said her character takes center stage in the film.
“This is Jessie’s story,” she told Britain’s Virgin Radio UK on May 28 in a joint interview with co-star Tim Allen. “It’s glorious.”
“This woman comes through,” Allen, who voices Buzz Lightyear, added.
Read on for the full lyrics to “I Knew It, I Knew You.”

title: Does ‘Masters of the Universe’ Have a Post-Credits Scene?
summary: “Masters of the Universe” star Alison Brie teases the possibility of a post-credits scene.
story: “Masters of the Universe” star Alison Brie is hinting that the movie may not actually be over when it ends.

“I always think for a movie of this kind you should stay through all of the credits. You never know what could happen,” Brie said on the 3rd hour of TODAY on June 4, alluding to the possibility of a post-credits scene.
“As someone who’s seen the film, I would also encourage viewers to do just that,” Craig Melvin added.

Brie portrays Skeletor’s second in command, Evil-Lyn, in the highly anticipated flick, which features Nicholas Galitzine tackling the role of He-Man, while Jared Leto, Idris Elba and Camila Mendes also star.

“Masters of the Universe” was a popular cartoon in the 1980s as well as a 1987 live-action film starring Dolph Lundgren, while other iterations of the franchise have also surfaced over the years. Brie was very aware of what it means to become a part of something with such a devoted following.
“It’s scary to step into such a big fandom sometimes,” she said. “You know, you want to make it your own, but honor the original.”
“Masters of the Universe” opened in theaters June 5.
Read on for what to know about the film after the credits roll.
Is There a Post-Credits Scene?
Spoiler alert: Yes, there is a post-credits scene. Actually, there are multiple ones that run during and after the credits.
What Happens During the Post-Credits Scenes?
Do not read on if you don’t want to know what to expect.
TODAY.com can confirm there’s a brief appearance by She-Ra, the long lost twin sister of Prince Adam, setting up the possibility of her in a sequel, or perhaps even her own movie.

There’s also a brief appearance featuring Orko, the floating magician, who informs audiences about the message to be taken  from the film, a throwback to the life lessons the cartoon series would include at the end of each episode.
The film also sets up the possibility of Skeletor returning to wreak more havoc, when Evil-Lyn takes his skull and comments he’s looked better before audiences hear him laugh.
Brie said on TODAY she was a fan of the cartoon growing up, although she said she wasn’t that familiar with Evil-Lyn.
“I remember as a child, sitting on the rug with my bowl of cereal, watching the cartoons, but I really didn’t know a ton about Evil-Lyn, and she is an amazing villain,” she said.

“Skeletor is kind of an all-time iconic villain, and so funny. And it was really fun to play Evil-Lyn, because they have a very contentious love-hate relationship. She’s always kind of annoyed by him and trying to sell him out, so there’s a lot of eye rolling that I get to do.”

title: Kate Middleton Comforts a Young Mother Who Completed Her Chemotherapy
summary: Catherine, Princess of Wales, supported a young English mom as she celebrated the completion of her chemotherapy treatment.
story: Catherine, Princess of Wales, supported a young English mom as she celebrated the completion of her chemotherapy treatment.

The former Kate Middleton, who has been in remission since January 2025, recently visited The Christie NHS Foundation Trust in Manchester, England. ITV Granada, a local British news channel, shared a video on Instagram June 4 of Kate’s heartfelt interaction with a 30-year-old mom named Claire Lorente, a patient in the hospital.
In the clip, the British royal walks into the cancer ward and tells Lorente, “Well done, you. Please, don’t worry. What a journey.”
Lorente becomes emotional as Kate asks, “It’s been a tough one?”
“It’s been hard,” Lorente says through tears.
Kate then continues to console Lorente and sweetly rubs her arm. “What an amazing thing. Isn’t mummy brave?” she asks Lorente’s toddler next to her.

The teary-eyed mom places her hands over her face as she is encouraged to ring a bell, marking the end of her treatment. Lorente walks over and rings the bell a few times as Kate and the hospital staff applaud her.
Kate reflected on her visit to The Christie, calling it “such a moving” experience, in a message shared on X.

“Meeting patients, families and staff was a powerful reminder that healing is about so much more than treatment alone,” she wrote. “A cancer diagnosis affects every part of life, in mind, body and the deeper part of ourselves. The way we process fear, uncertainty and change is deeply personal.”
She praised the hospital for taking care of its patients and for its commitment to improving their quality of life.

“These services empower patients to play an active role in their recovery and wellbeing, reminding us that care should always be (centered) on the whole person. Thank you for sharing your stories with me, and for the compassion, creativity and dedication shown every day across the hospital,” she added.
The post included a picture of Kate and Lorente meeting.

Kate has opened up about her cancer treatment over the years after first revealing her diagnosis in March 2024. She finished her chemotherapy treatment in September 2024, confirming that she was “cancer free.”
Last month, the princess flew to Italy in her first international trip since her recovery.
“She’s looking forward to being here, she’s energised, she’s enthused, she’s excited to see Reggio Emilia in action and meet the people here too,” Kate’s aide told NBC News.
The aide noted it was “a huge moment for the princess.”
“There will be many highlights of 2026 but this being her first international visit post her recovery... this is a really significant moment for her,” the aide said.

title: Elmo Just Wanted the Knicks and Spurs to Have Fun During the NBA Finals — Then Chaos Ensued
summary: Elmo cheered on the Spurs and Knicks ahead of Game 1, but people online were not happy with his message. See how Elmo reacted.
story: All Elmo wanted to do was make sure that both the New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurs enjoyed their time on the court as they kicked off the NBA Finals.
However, the furry red “Sesame Street” star was not expecting so many people to have strong opinions about his encouraging message.

Game 1 of the NBA Finals took place on June 3 at Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas. After an exciting game, the Knicks beat the Spurs 105-95, taking a 1-0 lead. Game 2 will take place on Friday, June 5.
In an attempt to join in the conversation, Elmo’s message of support rooting on both teams did not go as planned.
What Did Elmo Say About the NBA Finals?
On June 3, ahead of Game 1, Elmo’s official X account tweeted: “Elmo hopes both teams have fun!” It also included four basketball emoji.
How Did People React?
Generally, people were stunned that Elmo wouldn’t root for the Knicks. Among the comments were people mentioning that Sesame Street is in New York.
“Elmo, you’re from NYC, little buddy. It’s okay to root for the Knicks,” one person commented.

“Elmo, my guy  You’re literally red and furry from Sesame Street in the heart of NYC. The Knicks are down bad for decades  this is your moment to finally pick a side, little buddy,” another person replied. “We’ll forgive the neutrality if you rock the orange and blue tonight  Knicks in 6, let’s go.”
Others commented, “Elmo, you my mans but you can’t be fr rn,” “Elmo, you’re from the city. Pick a side man,” and “This isn’t the time for sportsmanship Elmo. These streets ain’t sesame.”
“Sesame Street is canonically located in New York City, which makes Elmo is a lifelong New Yorker,” one person wrote. “We all know you’re rooting for the Knicks Elmo.”
One person even shared the infamous Larry David, TODAY show moment in response.
The official Wendy’s account chimed in and in all caps wrote, “NOT NOW ELMO.”
There were some kind responses, which applauded his uplifting words.
“You get me, Elmo,” the official Sports account replied.
“What a nice thing to say, Elmo! I also hope both teams have fun, whether they be the winners OR the Spurs,” another fan tweeted.
Someone else also pointed out the angry messages, writing, “I hate how everyone is being mean to u elmo it’s a cruel world but dont let them dim your sparkle ok…..”
What Did Elmo Say in Response?
By June 4, Elmo had to speak out and responded in the most fitting and comical way.
“KNICKS that last message! Elmo didn’t mean to SPUR you on!” Elmo replied.

title: Hannah Berner Shares What She Told Ciara Miller Amid ‘Summer House’ Drama
summary: Hannah Berner is here to support Ciara Miller after all the “Summer House” drama.
story: Hannah Berner is here to support Ciara Miller after all the “Summer House” drama. She’s even looking out for her daily water intake.

Hannah appeared alongside Ciara in Season 5 of “Summer House” before departing the Bravo reality TV show. The two have stayed friends, most recently posing together for a selfie at the Las Culturistas Culture Awards in Los Angeles on May 30.

Appearing on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle June 4, Hannah gave an update on her friend amid the fallout from “Summer House” stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson going public with their relationship. (To recap: Ciara and West previously dated, and Ciara and Amanda were best friends.)
Asked for her thoughts on the drama, Hannah recalled the origin of her friendship with Ciara.

“Ciara’s great. She’s amazing. From Day 1, Ciara and I connected,” Hannah said on TODAY. “Even though the show definitely wanted us to not like each other. But you know what’s crazy? She walked in, she had a cat ring, and I said, ‘Forget the boys. Do you like cats?’ And she was like, ‘Yeah.’ I swear to god from that day forward ...”
After a brief interlude over Jenna and Hannah’s shared love of cats, the TODAY co-host got back on track.
“But so what ... that’s all the tea you’re going to give us?” Jenna prodded.

“The tea is that she’s doing great, she looks amazing, she’s having fun,” Hannah said. “It’s weird to see your friend go through something so intense because every time you go on the phone, it’s her face like doing something else.

“I was more just like, ‘Are you drinking enough water?’” Hannah added. “Like, ‘You’re on so many flights. You have a different brand deal every day.’ But I always told her that she’s a star. ... I love when my friends are successful because it makes me look cooler.”

In March, West and Amanda, who separated from husband and co-star Kyle Cooke in January, confirmed their “connection” after weeks of rumors. Ciara later said she read their statement “with the rest of the world” and reflected on experiencing “hurt” in the public eye in an interview with Glamour.
But it wasn’t until the “Summer House” reunion, which will air its third and final part on June 9, that viewers have seen Ciara confront Amanda face-to-face.

Hannah, who appeared in Seasons 3 through 5 of “Summer House” and currently co-hosts the pop culture podcast “Giggly Squad” with former co-star Paige DeSorbo, has previously spoken out about her support for Ciara, recalling how they were “supposed to be beefing” in Season 5 because they were interested in the same guy.
“The second I met her, I looked her in the eye and we were like, ‘I love you. You love cats. I f--- with you,’” Hannah said on “Giggly Squad” in April.
In addition to her podcast, Hannah is a comedian, and her latest comedy special “None of My Business,” will premiere on Hulu June 5.

title: TODAY Co-Hosts Debate Who Could Play Them In a Biopic. Vote on Their Picks!
summary: Savannah Guthrie, Craig Melvin, Carson Daly and  Dylan Dreyer discuss who should play them in movies about their lives.
story: They already have a place each day on the small screen, but who would play the TODAY co-hosts on the big screen?
After sharing news on June 4 that a biopic about music superstar Shania Twain is in the works, the TODAY crew began to imagine who might portray them in a movie.
Savannah Guthrie asked Craig Melvin who he would want to play him. Dylan Dreyer jumped in to suggest Idris Elba, which suited Craig just fine.
Craig then said Gwyneth Paltrow should play Savannah.
“That’d be nice, if she dyed her hair brown,” she replied, before Carson Daly floated Elizabeth Banks’ name.
“Oh, that’s nice! OK!” Savannah responded.
Savannah got Carson in on the act by saying Brendan Fraser can play him.
“Maybe,” Carson said.
“That’s good!” Craig exclaimed.
Carson, recalling Fraser’s role in “The Whale,”  joked about the choice, but Savannah explained that Fraser is “incredibly handsome.”
Dylan, meanwhile, already knew who she thinks should play her.
“In my mind, I always say Charlize Theron,” Dylan said,
“I want that for you,” Savannah said.
Later in the show, the co-hosts reacted to side-by-side photos of each other against their respective actor.
Vote on the team’s castings, below.
Savannah Guthrie Would Be Played by ... Gwyneth Paltrow!

title: While ‘Off Campus’ Viewers Are Just Meeting Briar OGs, the Author Is a Dozen Books Ahead — Writing About Their Children
summary: Elle Kennedy, the author of the “Off Campus” books that inspired the Prime Video show, spoke to TODAY.com about her new book in the Briar U universe.
story: Two months before “Off Campus” became Prime Video’s third most watched debut TV series, the story’s creator, Elle Kennedy, released a book following its main characters’ children.

Kennedy’s most recent novel, March 2026’s “Love Song,” follows the love story between Wyatt Graham — the son of Garrett Graham and Hannah Wells — and Blake Logan — the daughter of John Logan and his love interest (spoiler alert), Grace Ivers.
In Kennedy’s world, the beloved characters now being brought to life for the first time on screen are married with children. (She even has a whole webpage with detailed family trees here.)
But for newbies to the Briar University fictional universe, Garrett Graham and Hannah Wells, played by Belmont Cameli and Ella Bright, just met and fell in love.

“Seeing the response to the show and how people are resonating with Hannah and Garrett, and it’s this relationship that I came up with a decade ago, seeing it on screen and seeing it play out has just been so cool,” Kennedy tells TODAY.com.

Hannah and Garrett take center ice in Kennedy’s first story set at Briar University, 2015’s “The Deal.” Logan and Grace star in Book 2, “The Mistake” — though Allie (Mika Abdalla) and Dean’s (Stephen Kalyn) story will lead the show’s second season instead. (Logan is played by Antonio Cipriano and India Fowler has already been cast as Grace Ivers in a series regular role for Season 2.)

In the book world, Kennedy has written more than a dozen books set at or adjacent to Briar. Those include her “Off Campus” books, her “Briar U” books, her “Campus Diaries” books, “Love Song” and another standalone on the way. “Bad Idea,” starring AJ Connelly, son of “The Risk’s” Brenna Jensen (yes, Coach Jensen’s daughter) and then-Harvard superstar Jake Connelly, and Ivy Di Laurentis, one of Allie and Dean’s daughters, is set to publish in November 2026.
As the “Off Campus” TV universe is just beginning, Kennedy reflects on her characters coming to life and previews what’s ahead for the next generation.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

I think it was done so well. Their friendships and the girls’ as well, with Allie and Hannah, (it’s) not just that the actors love each other — because they do in real life, and it came across in the scenes as well — it’s like this is a family. It’s not just random college roommates. They’re all there for each other, which is exactly how it is in the books. Garrett and Logan are ride or die, and Tucker is more the nurturing presence in the house. And then same with Allie and Hannah. Allie just always has her back. So I think on screen that was conveyed so well.

The original ending, I think, I get why it was changed, and I was fine with the change, but I did kind of like playing up Garrett’s dad taking this extra-villainous role in the book. I do like that he pressured Hannah to exit Garrett’s life in a very mean, Phil way.

But again, it’s not something that I was like, “This is make or break.” I think the new ending works very well, and the dynamic with how Garrett views himself in this version of the story. I’m very happy with it. I think the adaptation came out great.

Oh, I knew about it, like, literally from before scripts were even written. When we had the big picture conversations with (showrunners) Louisa (Levy) and Gina (Fattore), they were kind of sharing, like, “This is how we view the Season 1 arc.” Right away, I believe Louisa was like, we know that Book 2 is Logan’s story, but because Allie is such a big part of Hannah’s life and she’s going to be in so many scenes, for television it probably makes more sense to then have her step up and be more of the focus.

So I knew about it from the start, and I supported that decision. And I think fans of “The Mistake” are still going to be happy because we have our Grace, we have our Logan, so it’s not like they’re not going to be in the show. They are. And everyone is going to get their moment.
I mean, you actually haven’t seen that much. It’s funny because in “The Score,” they are hooking up for probably half the book.

But you know, once their relationship kind of deepens ... there are so many plot points that are happening that we have not seen. Dean has an entire coaching story, Allie has her storyline with her career and the crossroads she’s at, where she wants to go in her story, in terms of acting.

And there’s obviously a big spoiler of another big, massive event that happens. So, I mean, we have eight episodes, and we have a lot of story left. ... I think they’re going to see some of their favorite moments that haven’t happened yet. There’s some scenes that you know they’re going to be getting teary-eyed over that are from the book.
There’s a lot more to come. I don’t think the whole story was already told.

I think that Allie and Dean, in their book, they’re able to find this deep connection with each other that neither of them expect with the other person. Especially for Dean. I think that the majority of “The Score” is more his journey, because Allie goes into it essentially knowing who she is, right? She might be fighting her nature a little bit, where it’s like, oh, I want to do drama, I want to do rom-com. But for the most part, she is confident. She’s confident in her body. She recognizes that she’s team boyfriend. She does struggle with the casual element of the relationship, and I think we saw that in Season 1 already, where she’s like, “I don’t do this, this hookup thing.”

So I want to see more that, both of them finding this deeper connection. Dean, especially, realizing the life of Dean is not all sunshine and rainbows, and kind of just growing as a person. So those are the kind of things I still would like to see. And how people deal with tragedy and changes and family issues, right? Whether her dad likes you or not — things like that, I’d like to see.

Hunter technically is in “Off Campus.” So I know we are one step closer now to some of the “Briar U” guys coming in. I think from “Briar U,” Summer (Di Laurentis’) love interest shows up, I believe he’s in Dean’s book quite a lot. And then Hunter is a big part of Dean’s story in “The Score.” So I was happy.

I was a little worried, because for new people who have not read the books, I was like, they’re going to be like, “Who’s Hunter Davenport?” But then, for people who have read the books, it was like a jaw-dropping, like, “Oh, my gosh, we got Hunter.”
So, yeah, I was happy to see him. I didn’t think we would see him this early, just because he doesn’t really become prominent until Book 3.
I’m all for it, and that means we’re one step closer to Jake (Connelly, lead of “The Risk”) who is my favorite “Briar U” hero, so hopefully we’ll see him.
I don’t know any details about that. This is just me as a fan of my own character.
I can’t give many spoilers because my publisher is like, we haven’t announced anything yet, not even tropes.
If you read “Love Song,” then you know that AJ is not in a great place because his girlfriend betrayed him with his best friend. Spoiler alert — but I mean the book’s been out long enough.

So we’re starting “Bad Idea” with him not being the greatest guy. I mean he’s a great guy, but like, he lost his best friend, he’s not in the best place, and the hockey program I can say is in shambles from something else.

So we start with him in that place. And Ivy is also starting from kind of a bad place, that her college experience has not gone at all how she thought it was going to. She was popular in high school, she had these great friends and this great family, and in her freshman year, she was instantly targeted by a group of people. So she’s dealing with bullying.
So they’re both out of sorts at the beginning of this book, and they end up becoming unlikely, I don’t want to say lovers, but they make an unlikely connection, and it’s not a good idea.

It’s not so much that it’s shifted. I’m still not a huge fan of the next-gen, because, again, I like the parental conflict. It really just depends on the character and on the story. So with Gigi and Ryder, I don’t know, they just made sense to me. None of the other characters called out to me at that time, right? So I did introduce her brother, and I introduced Blake, which is Logan’s daughter, but I just didn’t have a story for them.

And then I knew — OK, I have a real problem with wanting to kill off characters. Just because I used to write a lot of romantic suspense. So it was like I need to have a plot where I can do something. So in “The Dixon Rule,” I was able to kind of have these plot lines that I can’t do with the next-gen characters.

You can’t kill an original character, ever, and I will never do that. So fans rest assured, anytime a blurb says there’s a tragedy or there’s something, they immediately think I’m going to do something bad to one of their favorite characters. But none of the OGs will ever be harmed by me.

So yeah, it depends on the story and on the kind of conflict that I want to play with at the time. For “Campus Diaries,” there were things I wanted to do. I really wanted to tell an adoption story, so that was for “The Charlie Method,” and I’m like, “Oh, I can’t do that, because all my characters, we know who their parents are.”

So those ones I took a little detour, and I had a lot of fun writing them. And then for “Love Song,” that wasn’t even on my slate. I’m trying to remember what the next book was supposed to be, — it was supposed to be something completely different, and I was driving in the car, and I was like, “Oh, I think Wyatt should be with Blake.” And I instantly called my editor, and I was like, “Listen, I have an idea. I know it’s not the book we contracted, but I want them together. I think it’s going to be called ‘Love Song’ because he’s going to write a song about her.” And she’s like, “All right, great. So at the time of “The Graham Effect,” the plan was not to continue next-gen. It really just depends on the right story, if it hits me or not.

I think that the one that was very right was Ivy and AJ. Because that was always kind of my plan. I knew if I ever got to any of these younger next-gens, I thought they would be a good pairing, mostly because, again, I love Jake, so I’d love to see Jake and Brenna as parents, and then Dean is just always a delight to write, so I thought he would be fun as well. So that was the one that was right.
I don’t remember the one that was really off.
Maybe it was Blake and Beau? But I mean at the time, it could have gone either way.

title: ‘Friday Night Lights’ Showrunner ‘Really Tried’ to Write an Affair for Eric, Tami Taylor. The Actors Refused.
summary: “Friday Night Lights” stars Kyle Chandler and Connie Britton reunite to discuss the show’s legacy and its iconic phrase: “Clear eyes, full hearts, can’t lose.”
story: The “Friday Night Lights” showrunner attempted to write at a controversial storyline for Eric and Tami Taylor that could have redefined how fans remember their relationship between the iconic TV couple.

Head writer and executive producer Jason Katims said in an interview that aired on TODAY June 4 that the creative team tried to write an affair plot between coach Taylor and his wife, Tami. But actors Kyle Chandler and Connie Britton refused to portray it.
“We agreed right at the outset, we’re like, ‘No matter what, we can’t let them make either one of us have an affair,’” Britton said.
“We tried,” Katims chimed in, shaking his head.

“They really tried,” Britton added. “We pushed right back on it. We wanted (the Taylor marriage) to be about partnership and just collaboration, and making mistakes, but always going back to each other.”

NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz spoke with the cast and showrunner in Austin, Texas — where the show was filmed — while the group was in town for a 20th anniversary celebration of the show at the ATX TV Festival on May 29. The reunion was also part of NBC100, celebrating the broadcast network’s 100th anniversary.
The chemistry that made Chandler and Britton’s characters such a beloved television couple was on display during the interview.
“It’s kinda miserable right now, sitting next to her,” Chandler joked, referring to Britton.
Chandler and Britton were joined by Aimee Teegarden, Scott Porter, Adrianne Palicki, Gaius Charles, Louanne Stephens and Jesse Plemons for a panel at the festival and Schwartz’s interview.
Speaking with NBC News, Chandler expounded on the classic phrase his character used to exhort his football players at the fictional Dillon High School in Texas: “Clear eyes, full hearts, can’t lose.”

Coach Taylor was tough, but he always encouraged his players to be the best versions of themselves. His signature line often concluded his motivational locker room speeches before the Panthers took the field.
Reflecting on the phrase, Chandler told Schwartz that he interprets the message to be about having upstanding character.
“It means be smart. Keep your eyes open. Keep your heart open, and you can’t lose,” he explained, adding that the phrase is also about honesty and integrity.
“He didn’t write his own lines, you see,” Britton jokingly chimed in.
“I thought that was (a) pretty good answer,” he replied.
Speaking about their time on set, Britton highlighted the feelings of pride and “ownership” cast members felt toward their characters.

“We all were given such a sense of ownership of the show,” she said. “We all felt a sense like, ‘This character is ours, and it is a privilege for us to be able to create it.’ But we didn’t just feel that way. Everybody felt like they were part of something that they believed in.”
She previously said she and Chandler were able to make the “no cheating” request a rule for their characters.
“We were like, ‘We’re never letting the writers have us have an affair. If they try to do it, we’re not going to do it,’” she told Entertainment Weekly in 2017.
Chandler also complimented the cast’s teamwork in the interview with Schwartz.
“From the very bottom of the call list to the very top of the call list, it was one organism that worked together,” he recalled. “It was a magical. As they say, lightning in a bottle.”
To capture that magic one more time, Schwartz asked Chandler if he could lead the group in a chant.
“Coach, if you don’t mind, can you cue us up with just the first two?” he asked.
Chandler shifted into coach Taylor mode and cheered, “Clear eyes, full hearts ...”
“Can’t lose!” everyone answered in unison.

title: Stranger Things’ Creators’ New Show, No. 1 on Netflix, Is a Sci-Fi Series About Senior Citizens
summary: What is The Boroughs about? It’s the top show on Netflix. Here’s what to know about the top charts.
story: Matt and Ross Duffer’s show “Stranger Things” was a blockbuster hit, running for nine years on Netflix and culminating in an emotional finale last December. Six months later, the Duffer brothers are back with “The Boroughs,” a show that blends sci-fi and heart, and fans are all over it.

The eight-episode series secured the No. 1 spot on the Netflix English TV series chart to kick off June, following its May 21 debut, Netflix reports. It’s also the top show in 25 countries, per Netflix.

With the new sci-fi horror series, creators Jeffrey Addiss and Will Matthews, alongside “Stranger Things” creators Matt and Ross Duffer as executive producers, took their imagination far from the fictional Hawkins, Indiana of “Stranger Things.”
“The Boroughs” tells the story of a group of senior citizens who reside at a peculiar, upscale retirement community in the desolate outskirts of the New Mexico desert.

This retiree group is comprised of protagonist Sam Cooper (Alfred Molina), Renee Joyce (Geena Davis), Judy Daniels (Alfre Woodard) and her husband, Art Daniels (Clarke Peters), Wally Baker (Denis O’Hare), and Jack Willard (Bill Pullman).
The series follows Sam’s reluctant move to the community and discovery that stranger things are happening here than dentures going missing.
This group may be eligible for AARP benefits, but they fight back with force against the supernatural, extraterrestrial activity that becomes toxic to their community.
“Nemesis” was dethroned to the No. 2 spot with 5.9 million views, compared to a 9.6 million views for “The Boroughs.”

Despite a cryptic cliffhanger, there has been no confirmation from Netflix on a Season 2. Fans of “The Boroughs” can remain hopeful, however, as the creative team did create the show with a three-season plan in mind, showrunner Jeffrey Addiss told IGN.
“We think we know the last shot of the last scene of the last episode,” Addiss said.
Davis, best known for her iconic role of Thelma in “Thelma & Louise,” spoke highly of the project and her costars.
“It’s such a special project, so well-written,” the Academy Award-winning actor told LA Times. “And I just love the ensemble.”
Just as he and Matthews manifested collaborating with the Duffer Brothers, Addiss told the publication he had Davis in mind early on.
“Geena was one of the people we wanted from the beginning,” Addiss told LA Times. “When we met with her, she was so excited. She said, ‘I want to be rock and roll and kill people.’”
In “The Boroughs,” Davis and the rest of the cast do just that. Now, fans will wait to learn if the series will share the same vitality as its characters — the lucky ones, at least.

title: Tina Fey on ‘The Four Seasons’ and Why Being in Her 50s is Like ‘Reverse Puberty’
summary: Tina Fey sits down to talk about Season 2 of the Netflix show, “The Four Seasons,” how much of it is based on real life and if there will be a Season 3.
story: It’s no secret that many of Tina Fey’s most successful projects are rooted in personal experience. Her latest, the hit Netflix series “The Four Seasons,” is no exception.

“If I think of writing ‘Mean Girls’ way, way, way back the first time, that was definitely like reflecting on high school experiences as I knew them. Then writing ‘30 Rock,’ that was like the middle period where life was all work,” the “SNL” alum says during a sit-down interview with TODAY.com.
“(‘The Four Seasons’) is a reflection now of not only sort of where I’m at, but also like, of the kind of show I want to watch now,” Fey, 56, adds.

In addition to Fey, the feel-good dramedy stars Will Forte, Kerri Kenney-Silver, Colman Domingo, Erika Henningsen, Marco Calvani and Steve Carell as a group of friends who vacation together every season, chronicling the ups and downs of their respective relationships.

Set in a variety of locations, including New York’s Hudson Valley, Puerto Rico and Italy, “The Four Seasons” is an adaptation of the 1981 film. The original stars Carol Burnett and Alan Alda as a New York City couple navigating married life as their circle of friends sort through their own relationship issues.

Fey, a fan of Alda’s, saw the film when she was 10 years old. It left its mark, inspiring her, along with writers Lang Fisher and Tracey Wigfield, to turn the classic rom-com into a series decades later.
When the trio began collaborating on a new project, “(Tina) kept saying, ‘I want it to be like ‘The Four Seasons,’” Fisher said in an interview with Netflix’s Tudum.
“Finally we were like, ‘Should we just watch ‘The Four Seasons’? We did, and then we were like, ‘Oh, we should just do that movie,’” she added.
Little White Lies

The show, now in its second season, pays tribute to the ’80s film with plenty of Easter eggs, including a two-episode cameo from Alda in Season 1 and the resurrection of one of Burnett’s most memorable lines.
“I hate to break it to you, but you’re married to a middle-aged woman with dry skin,” Fey’s character, Kate, exasperatedly utters to hubby Jack (Forte).
Aging, like relationships, is front and center in “The Four Seasons,” with each of the main characters facing their own reckoning with mortality.
Fey says many of the references and moments woven into the plot of are, in fact, based on the real-life experiences of the series’ writers.
“We’re all pretty much the same age,” Fey says of the creative team.

“Almost all of us have known each other a super long time, and so all the writers are really good about sharing their own things,” she adds. “Then sometimes our spouses are like, ‘Is that me?’ And then we’ll usually just be like, ‘No, no.’ We’ll just lie and say, ‘That’s so and so.’”

White lies aside, Fey says that the writers are “generous” about sharing their personal anecdotes. Much like the characters in “The Four Seasons,” she and her cohorts have essentially been schooled by life.
“The peaks and valleys have really flattened out,” Fey says. “I’m just like, ‘OK. That happened? OK. We won a prize? OK. There was a fire? OK.’ Like you just ... flat,” she says.
She agrees that, like most things, it’s hard to truly understand what it means to age until it happens to you.

“I feel like it’s a reverse puberty in a way. It’s like your body starts to change and be disgusting in new ways,” she says with a laugh. “When you’re 12 or 13, you’re like, ‘Why am I so oily?’ And now you’re like, ‘Why am I so papery?’”
Will There Be a Season 3 of ‘The Four Seasons’?
With another season of “The Four Seasons” comes the chance to reunite with longtime friends and colleagues.

“We know each other even better now,” Fey says. “I can’t believe Kerri Kenney-Silver and I did not know each other before this because we’re the same age and the same comedy generation, so it’s really nice to count her as a friend now that I feel like we really are getting to know each other.”
Fey also again teams up with Erika Henningsen, who starred as Cady Heron in the original Broadway version of “Mean Girls,” which Fey wrote.
“I’ve actually known her for 10 years, which is crazy,” Fey says.
“We fell into an algorithm Season 1 and it was even easier to jump back in Season 2,” Fey adds of working on the latest season, which hit Netflix May 28.
Could the show return for Season 3?
“We hope there will be,” Fey says. “We’re certainly all up for it if there will be.”
Given the show is currently at No. 1 on Netflix, the “30 Rock” actor says it’s a “good sign” that the show will probably return. She anticipates a decision being made later this month.
Either way, Fey is keeping an open mind about the future.
“I’m hoping that things will just continue to present themselves. There definitely are other forms of writing that still interest me. I’d love to write a play,” she says.
“I’d love to maybe write in prose in another form,” she adds. “But I also just like sitting still in a chair and drinking coffee outside.”

title: Marcello Hernández to Bring the Laughs as Host of the 2026 ESPYS
summary: Marcello Hernández is taking over the ESPYS. The “Saturday Night Live” star will host the annual sports awards show.
story: Marcello Hernández is taking over the ESPYS.
The “Saturday Night Live” star is bringing the laughs to the annual sports awards show after officially being named the host of the 2026 ESPYS.

“I started doing comedy 10 years ago, in Cleveland, Ohio, and I would take the train 12 hours to New York to sell comedy tickets on the street in Greenwich Village in exchange for stage time,” said Hernández in a press release. “It is an honor, and frankly feels crazy to be hosting the ESPYS this year in New York. I’m sure the energy is going to be great.”

The 2026 ESPYS, presented by Capital One, will take place on Wednesday, July 15, and air live at 8 p.m. on ABC. The ceremony will be held from the David H. Koch Theater at Lincoln Center in New York City.

Previous hosts include Jimmy Kimmel, John Cena, LeBron James, Serena Williams, Shane Gillis and many others. The ESPYS honor the major sports achievements of the past year. The show also celebrates unforgettable moments and recognizes notable athletes and sports figures who dominated during their seasons.

Hernández’s hosting gig comes after he performed his biggest show in Spanish during the Netflix Is A Joke festival. During the event, which was held on Mother’s Day, the comedian took a moment to honor his most loyal supporter: his mom Isabel Cancela.
“God bless all the moms out there tonight who have cared for their kids, made sacrifices,” he said in Spanish, with his mom by his side. “Today, we celebrate you.”
“God gave me a mom that worked every day for me and today I thank her and everyone who is here,” he continued.

Hernández previously shared during a January appearance on TODAY that his mom is his muse, crediting her for a lot of his material. He added that his Netflix standup, “Marcello Hernández: American Boy,” was a way for him to give her much-deserved flowers.

title: Carson Daly Chugs Beer Out Of His Shoe Live On TODAY
summary: Carson Daly tried a “shoey” live on TODAY June 3 and chugged beer out of his shoe, inspired by Ryan Reynolds and Hugh Jackman.
story: It’s not uncommon for TODAY co-hosts to try fun challenges live on air. But chugging a beer from a shoe?
TODAY co-host Carson Daly attempted his inaugural “shoey” on June 3 after watching Ryan Reynolds and Hugh Jackman partake in the beloved Australian tradition in a clip Jackman shared on Instagram.
Carson told his co-hosts he had never done a “shoey” as he started unlacing his loafers.
“Let’s go,” Jenna Bush Hager said before Carson pulled out a beer bottle from inside his suit jacket. He proceeded to pull off his shoe and pour the drink inside.
Carson chugged it down in seconds as Sheinelle Jones, Savannah Guthrie and Jenna cheered him on.
While cringing, Craig Melvin called Daly’s feat “impressive.”
Later on the plaza, Carson again brought up the “shoey.”
“Next time I’m gonna drink it out of Savannah’s shoe, and I’m gonna call it a ‘Choo-y,’” he said, referencing her Jimmy Choo heels.
Savannah offered for Carson to take on the “Choo-y” challenge right then and there, holding out her heel.
“Do you know what I have to do to stick around with these fine, hard-hitting journalists?” Carson joked. “Some interview presidents, some do ‘shoeys’ and ‘Choo-ys.’ Gotta earn your keep, everybody.”
The original clip that inspired Carson’s antics saw the “Deadpool and Wolverine” co-stars chug down their own “shoeys” with the Australian sailing team the duo recently purchased: Bonds Flying Roos.
“I would say it’s been a good 35 years since my last shoey!!!” Jackman captioned the video.

The Bonds Flying Roos will be the focus of a new Disney+ docuseries announced May 29, which will follow Reynolds, Jackman and the team through a global racing season “defined by speed, pressure and high stakes,” per a press release.
The series is the pair’s first collaboration since “Deadpool & Wolverine.”
“We once again anticipate action, comedy, heart but with a lot more water,” Jackman and Reynolds said in a statement. “And (fingers crossed) pirates.”

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Power Rankings: The Premiere’s Winners, Losers and Surprises
summary: TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin share their thoughts on the premiere.
story: In TODAY.com’s new weekly “Love Island USA” column, content creators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin break down the latest group of Season 8 episodes, sharing their thoughts on the new bombshells, couplings and dumping. Check back here over the next six weeks to read the duo’s hot takes and follow @tessdhiggins and @sallydarrgriffin on their socials to keep up with them throughout the week.
“The summer has officially begun!” host Ariana Madix announces to welcome viewers to “Love Island USA” Season 8.

The supersized premiere kicked off with an elaborate music video set to “Say You Do” by Sigala, Imani Williams and DJ Fresh that featured the first 10 islanders and Madix. Next, the cast made their way into the Fijian villa and introduced themselves to each other in the nearly two-hour episode. After the islanders shared brief facts, it was time for the first game. The islanders tested their compatibility and chemistry with each other in a game that ended with everyone making out multiple times.
Then, it was time to couple up, forming the first couples of the season. Without further ado, the couples are…
Trinity and Bryce
Trinity and Bryce
Melanie and Sincere
Melanie and Sincere
Kenzie and Zach
Kenzie and Zach
Amiya and KC
Amiya and KC
Beatriz and Sean
Beatriz and Sean
How are TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin feeling about Season 8’s potential?

“This episode was an hour and 45 minutes. It was basically a movie and I did enjoy every single second of it because I love ‘Love Island.’ I’m so excited that it’s back!” Sally tells TODAY.com about the first episode. She adds that the extended premiere allowed fans to really get to know the islanders instantly.
Tess was also a fan of the episode and shares why one islander’s decision in a game could cost them a spot in the villa.
Find out what Tess and Sally thought about the initial pairs and the rest of the steamy premiere, including the two bombshells.
Best Moment

Tess: “I always love the girls getting a chance to connect alone and with Ariana (Madix) before the guys come in, because we love seeing the friendships develop on this show as the audience. It really sets the tone nicely for the group to get excited and get to know each other, like even outside of the couples.”

Sally: “I would say the bombshells coming in at the end. I was anticipating it the entire time. That’s always how a great first episode should end. It shocks the audience and it shocks the Islanders. It’s so fun to see the look on everyone’s faces. And they do it differently every time and I think the game that they played bringing them in was so fun.”
Biggest Surprise

Tess: “No one standing behind KC’s door to match with him. Maybe this was because of his, you know, ‘We all like to work out, right?’ comment that he made. Because I could see that rubbing people the wrong way, perhaps. Curious to see where things go for him.”

Sally: “I would say in the last game that they played where each person in the couple got to decide if they wanted to stay or switch. Both Trinity and Bryce decided to switch, which I thought was interesting because I thought they were having a good time. They were getting to know each other and I didn’t expect for both of them to decide to switch it up. Then also, Aniya and KC both stayed. I thought that was interesting because in the original coupling game they got together by default, and so I thought it was kind of sweet and then they decided to stay and get to know each other. I’m excited to see where they go.”
Best Couple
Tess: “So far, I’m getting a good vibe from Melanie and Sincere. They seem like a good early match to me. I think she was charmed by him, and they do have that east coast connection.”
Sally: “Melanie and Sincere. They seemed to really like each other. They’re vibing and they seem to be the strongest ones right now.”
Biggest Winner

Tess: “I thought Trinity was really funny this episode. She comes across really carefree and unafraid to speak her mind or make a bold choice. I’m also excited to see where things go for her, especially as the youngest member of the cast so far. And being paired up right now with a guy who is much older than her.”

Sally: “I would say Melanie and Sincere because they do seem to have a thing going and I’m really excited for them. I would say that they would probably be the public’s favorite at this point, so they’re the biggest winner in that sense. Then I would say also Zach because his brother is Charlie (Georgiou) from last season. All of these people are new to us, but Zach kind of has a little bit of an advantage because we feel like we know him. We know his brother and he also really looks like his brother.”
At Risk of Being Dumped

Tess: “Kenzie. You know, Zach was really giving her friend-only vibes, and there doesn’t seem to be anyone else she was connecting with right now. Seemingly she was super excited about him and into him, and it just wasn’t giving a match. Not sure where that leaves her.”

Sally: “It is early days. You never know with ‘Love Island.’ People could be dumped tomorrow. I hope not. I hope people get to stick around a little bit longer that we just got to know over the course of an hour and 45 minutes. But I would say Kenzie and Sean because they were the two people that were left alone at that final stay or switch it up game.”

title: Did the ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Premiere Address Vasana Montgomery’s Departure?
summary: How the Season 8 premiere of “Love Island USA” addressed Vasana Montgomery’s early departure following scandal.
story: Season 8 of “Love Island USA” began with a scandal.

Prior to the premiere on Tuesday, June 2, Peacock shared the identities of the 12 original islanders entering the villa in the first episode. But before ever seeing the cast members interact, viewers learned that one contestant had already exited the show.

Vasana Montgomery, a 25-year-old business owner from Beaverton, Oregon, was featured in the introduction video released May 28 that revealed the cast. She spoke about what she was hoping to find in the villa. But shortly after the clip was shared, fans found social media videos of Montgomery appearing to say a racial slur on camera. In one video, she seemed to say the N-word while singing a song, in another she said the same slur while playing an arcade game, according to NBC News.
On May 29, she was removed from the cast and Peacock confirmed to NBC News that she will no longer be on the reality dating show. (Peacock is owned by NBCUniversal, the parent company of TODAY.com.)
Montgomery did not respond to NBC News’ request for comment following news of her removal.

When the premiere began June 2, Montgomery was not featured at all. Instead, the episode introduced 10 islanders. The first batch of women islanders included Aniya Harvey, Beatriz Hatz, Kenzie Annis, Melanie Moreno and Trinity Tatum.
Later, two bombshells entered the villa: Gabriel Vasconcelos, who was previously revealed last week, and Kayda Bosse.

Montgomery’s exit comes after two Season 7 islanders were removed from the villa last year after they had problematic videos resurface on social media, as well.
